<task id="_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml" name="Execute Workflow" internal="true">
  <objective>Execute given workflow by loading its configuration, following instructions, and producing output</objective>

  <llm critical="true">
    <mandate>Always read COMPLETE files - NEVER use offset/limit when reading any workflow related files</mandate>
    <mandate>Instructions are MANDATORY - either as file path, steps or embedded list in YAML, XML or markdown</mandate>
    <mandate>Execute ALL steps in instructions IN EXACT ORDER</mandate>
    <mandate>Save to template output file after EVERY "template-output" tag</mandate>
    <mandate>NEVER skip a step - YOU are responsible for every steps execution without fail or excuse</mandate>
  </llm>

  <WORKFLOW-RULES critical="true">
    <rule n="1">Steps execute in exact numerical order (1, 2, 3...)</rule>
    <rule n="2">Optional steps: Ask user unless #yolo mode active</rule>
    <rule n="3">Template-output tags: Save content, discuss with the user the section completed, and NEVER proceed until the users indicates
      to proceed (unless YOLO mode has been activated)</rule>
  </WORKFLOW-RULES>

  <flow>
    <step n="1" title="Load and Initialize Workflow">
      <substep n="1a" title="Load Configuration and Resolve Variables">
        <action>Read workflow.yaml from provided path</action>
        <mandate>Load config_source (REQUIRED for all modules)</mandate>
        <phase n="1">Load external config from config_source path</phase>
        <phase n="2">Resolve all {config_source}: references with values from config</phase>
        <phase n="3">Resolve system variables (date:system-generated) and paths ({project-root}, {installed_path})</phase>
        <phase n="4">Ask user for input of any variables that are still unknown</phase>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1b" title="Load Required Components">
        <mandate>Instructions: Read COMPLETE file from path OR embedded list (REQUIRED)</mandate>
        <check>If template path → Read COMPLETE template file</check>
        <check>If validation path → Note path for later loading when needed</check>
        <check>If template: false → Mark as action-workflow (else template-workflow)</check>
        <note>Data files (csv, json) → Store paths only, load on-demand when instructions reference them</note>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1c" title="Initialize Output" if="template-workflow">
        <action>Resolve default_output_file path with all variables and {{date}}</action>
        <action>Create output directory if doesn't exist</action>
        <action>If template-workflow → Write template to output file with placeholders</action>
        <action>If action-workflow → Skip file creation</action>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="2" title="Process Each Instruction Step in Order">
      <iterate>For each step in instructions:</iterate>

      <substep n="2a" title="Handle Step Attributes">
        <check>If optional="true" and NOT #yolo → Ask user to include</check>
        <check>If if="condition" → Evaluate condition</check>
        <check>If for-each="item" → Repeat step for each item</check>
        <check>If repeat="n" → Repeat step n times</check>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2b" title="Execute Step Content">
        <action>Process step instructions (markdown or XML tags)</action>
        <action>Replace {{variables}} with values (ask user if unknown)</action>
        <execute-tags>
          <tag>action xml tag → Perform the action</tag>
          <tag>check if="condition" xml tag → Conditional block wrapping actions (requires closing </check>)</tag>
          <tag>ask xml tag → Prompt user and WAIT for response</tag>
          <tag>invoke-workflow xml tag → Execute another workflow with given inputs and the workflow.xml runner</tag>
          <tag>invoke-task xml tag → Execute specified task</tag>
          <tag>invoke-protocol name="protocol_name" xml tag → Execute reusable protocol from protocols section</tag>
          <tag>goto step="x" → Jump to specified step</tag>
        </execute-tags>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2c" title="Handle template-output Tags">
        <if tag="template-output">
          <mandate>Generate content for this section</mandate>
          <mandate>Save to file (Write first time, Edit subsequent)</mandate>
          <action>Display generated content</action>
          <ask> [a] Advanced Elicitation, [c] Continue, [p] Party-Mode, [y] YOLO the rest of this document only. WAIT for response. <if
              response="a">
              <action>Start the advanced elicitation workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/advanced-elicitation/workflow.xml</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="c">
              <action>Continue to next step</action>
            </if>
            <if response="p">
              <action>Start the party-mode workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/party-mode/workflow.md</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="y">
              <action>Enter #yolo mode for the rest of the workflow</action>
            </if>
          </ask>
        </if>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2d" title="Step Completion">
        <check>If no special tags and NOT #yolo:</check>
        <ask>Continue to next step? (y/n/edit)</ask>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="3" title="Completion">
      <check>Confirm document saved to output path</check>
      <action>Report workflow completion</action>
    </step>
  </flow>

  <execution-modes>
    <mode name="normal">Full user interaction and confirmation of EVERY step at EVERY template output - NO EXCEPTIONS except yolo MODE</mode>
    <mode name="yolo">Skip all confirmations and elicitation, minimize prompts and try to produce all of the workflow automatically by
      simulating the remaining discussions with an simulated expert user</mode>
  </execution-modes>

  <supported-tags desc="Instructions can use these tags">
    <structural>
      <tag>step n="X" goal="..." - Define step with number and goal</tag>
      <tag>optional="true" - Step can be skipped</tag>
      <tag>if="condition" - Conditional execution</tag>
      <tag>for-each="collection" - Iterate over items</tag>
      <tag>repeat="n" - Repeat n times</tag>
    </structural>
    <execution>
      <tag>action - Required action to perform</tag>
      <tag>action if="condition" - Single conditional action (inline, no closing tag needed)</tag>
      <tag>check if="condition">...</check> - Conditional block wrapping multiple items (closing tag required)</tag>
      <tag>ask - Get user input (ALWAYS wait for response before continuing)</tag>
      <tag>goto - Jump to another step</tag>
      <tag>invoke-workflow - Call another workflow</tag>
      <tag>invoke-task - Call a task</tag>
      <tag>invoke-protocol - Execute a reusable protocol (e.g., discover_inputs)</tag>
    </execution>
    <output>
      <tag>template-output - Save content checkpoint</tag>
      <tag>critical - Cannot be skipped</tag>
      <tag>example - Show example output</tag>
    </output>
  </supported-tags>

  <protocols desc="Reusable workflow protocols that can be invoked via invoke-protocol tag">
    <protocol name="discover_inputs" desc="Smart file discovery and loading based on input_file_patterns">
      <objective>Intelligently load project files (whole or sharded) based on workflow's input_file_patterns configuration</objective>

      <critical>Only execute if workflow.yaml contains input_file_patterns section</critical>

      <flow>
        <step n="1" title="Parse Input File Patterns">
          <action>Read input_file_patterns from loaded workflow.yaml</action>
          <action>For each pattern group (prd, architecture, epics, etc.), note the load_strategy if present</action>
        </step>

        <step n="2" title="Load Files Using Smart Strategies">
          <iterate>For each pattern in input_file_patterns:</iterate>

          <substep n="2a" title="Try Sharded Documents First">
            <check if="sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Determine load_strategy from pattern config (defaults to FULL_LOAD if not specified)</action>

              <strategy name="FULL_LOAD">
                <desc>Load ALL files in sharded directory - used for PRD, Architecture, UX, brownfield docs</desc>
                <action>Use glob pattern to find ALL .md files (e.g., "{output_folder}/*architecture*/*.md")</action>
                <action>Load EVERY matching file completely</action>
                <action>Concatenate content in logical order (index.md first if exists, then alphabetical)</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="SELECTIVE_LOAD">
                <desc>Load specific shard using template variable - example: used for epics with {{epic_num}}</desc>
                <action>Check for template variables in sharded_single pattern (e.g., {{epic_num}})</action>
                <action>If variable undefined, ask user for value OR infer from context</action>
                <action>Resolve template to specific file path</action>
                <action>Load that specific file</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="INDEX_GUIDED">
                <desc>Load index.md, analyze structure and description of each doc in the index, then intelligently load relevant docs</desc>
                <mandate>DO NOT BE LAZY - use best judgment to load documents that might have relevant information, even if only a 5% chance</mandate>
                <action>Load index.md from sharded directory</action>
                <action>Parse table of contents, links, section headers</action>
                <action>Analyze workflow's purpose and objective</action>
                <action>Identify which linked/referenced documents are likely relevant</action>
                <example>If workflow is about authentication and index shows "Auth Overview", "Payment Setup", "Deployment" → Load auth
                  docs, consider deployment docs, skip payment</example>
                <action>Load all identified relevant documents</action>
                <action>Store combined content in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
                <note>When in doubt, LOAD IT - context is valuable, being thorough is better than missing critical info</note>
              </strategy>
              <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2b" title="Try Whole Document if No Sharded Found">
            <check if="no sharded matches found OR no sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Attempt glob match on 'whole' pattern (e.g., "{output_folder}/*prd*.md")</action>
              <check if="matches found">
                <action>Load ALL matching files completely (no offset/limit)</action>
                <action>Store content in variable: {pattern_name_content} (e.g., {prd_content})</action>
                <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
              </check>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2c" title="Handle Not Found">
            <check if="no matches for sharded OR whole">
              <action>Set {pattern_name_content} to empty string</action>
              <action>Note in session: "No {pattern_name} files found" (not an error, just unavailable, offer use change to provide)</action>
            </check>
          </substep>
        </step>

        <step n="3" title="Report Discovery Results">
          <action>List all loaded content variables with file counts</action>
          <example>

<workflow>
  <critical>The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml</critical>
  <critical>You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml</critical>
  <critical>Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and language MUST be tailored to {user_skill_level}</critical>
  <critical>Generate all documents in {document_output_language}</critical>
  <critical>Only modify the story file in these areas: Tasks/Subtasks checkboxes, Dev Agent Record (Debug Log, Completion Notes), File List,
    Change Log, and Status</critical>
  <critical>Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps</critical>
  <critical>Absolutely DO NOT stop because of "milestones", "significant progress", or "session boundaries". Continue in a single execution
    until the story is COMPLETE (all ACs satisfied and all tasks/subtasks checked) UNLESS a HALT condition is triggered or the USER gives
    other instruction.</critical>
  <critical>Do NOT schedule a "next session" or request review pauses unless a HALT condition applies. Only Step 6 decides completion.</critical>
  <critical>User skill level ({user_skill_level}) affects conversation style ONLY, not code updates.</critical>

  <step n="1" goal="Find next ready story and load it" tag="sprint-status">
    <check if="{{story_path}} is provided">
      <action>Use {{story_path}} directly</action>
      <action>Read COMPLETE story file</action>
      <action>Extract story_key from filename or metadata</action>
      <goto anchor="task_check" />
    </check>

    <!-- Sprint-based story discovery -->
    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file exists">
      <critical>MUST read COMPLETE sprint-status.yaml file from start to end to preserve order</critical>
      <action>Load the FULL file: {{sprint_status}}</action>
      <action>Read ALL lines from beginning to end - do not skip any content</action>
      <action>Parse the development_status section completely to understand story order</action>

      <action>Find the FIRST story (by reading in order from top to bottom) where:
        - Key matches pattern: number-number-name (e.g., "1-2-user-auth")
        - NOT an epic key (epic-X) or retrospective (epic-X-retrospective)
        - Status value equals "ready-for-dev"
      </action>

      <check if="no ready-for-dev or in-progress story found">
        <output>📋 No ready-for-dev stories found in sprint-status.yaml

          **Current Sprint Status:** {{sprint_status_summary}}

          **What would you like to do?**
          1. Run `create-story` to create next story from epics with comprehensive context
          2. Run `*validate-create-story` to improve existing stories before development (recommended quality check)
          3. Specify a particular story file to develop (provide full path)
          4. Check {{sprint_status}} file to see current sprint status

          💡 **Tip:** Stories in `ready-for-dev` may not have been validated. Consider running `validate-create-story` first for a quality
          check.
        </output>
        <ask>Choose option [1], [2], [3], or [4], or specify story file path:</ask>

        <check if="user chooses '1'">
          <action>HALT - Run create-story to create next story</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '2'">
          <action>HALT - Run validate-create-story to improve existing stories</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '3'">
          <ask>Provide the story file path to develop:</ask>
          <action>Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}</action>
          <goto anchor="task_check" />
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '4'">
          <output>Loading {{sprint_status}} for detailed status review...</output>
          <action>Display detailed sprint status analysis</action>
          <action>HALT - User can review sprint status and provide story path</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user provides story file path">
          <action>Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}</action>
          <goto anchor="task_check" />
        </check>
      </check>
    </check>

    <!-- Non-sprint story discovery -->
    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file does NOT exist">
      <action>Search {story_dir} for stories directly</action>
      <action>Find stories with "ready-for-dev" status in files</action>
      <action>Look for story files matching pattern: *-*-*.md</action>
      <action>Read each candidate story file to check Status section</action>

      <check if="no ready-for-dev stories found in story files">
        <output>📋 No ready-for-dev stories found

          **Available Options:**
          1. Run `create-story` to create next story from epics with comprehensive context
          2. Run `*validate-create-story` to improve existing stories
          3. Specify which story to develop
        </output>
        <ask>What would you like to do? Choose option [1], [2], or [3]:</ask>

        <check if="user chooses '1'">
          <action>HALT - Run create-story to create next story</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '2'">
          <action>HALT - Run validate-create-story to improve existing stories</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '3'">
          <ask>It's unclear what story you want developed. Please provide the full path to the story file:</ask>
          <action>Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}</action>
          <action>Continue with provided story file</action>
        </check>
      </check>

      <check if="ready-for-dev story found in files">
        <action>Use discovered story file and extract story_key</action>
      </check>
    </check>

    <action>Store the found story_key (e.g., "1-2-user-authentication") for later status updates</action>
    <action>Find matching story file in {story_dir} using story_key pattern: {{story_key}}.md</action>
    <action>Read COMPLETE story file from discovered path</action>

    <anchor id="task_check" />

    <action>Parse sections: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Dev Agent Record, File List, Change Log, Status</action>

    <action>Load comprehensive context from story file's Dev Notes section</action>
    <action>Extract developer guidance from Dev Notes: architecture requirements, previous learnings, technical specifications</action>
    <action>Use enhanced story context to inform implementation decisions and approaches</action>

    <action>Identify first incomplete task (unchecked [ ]) in Tasks/Subtasks</action>

    <action if="no incomplete tasks">
      <goto step="6">Completion sequence</goto>
    </action>
    <action if="story file inaccessible">HALT: "Cannot develop story without access to story file"</action>
    <action if="incomplete task or subtask requirements ambiguous">ASK user to clarify or HALT</action>
  </step>

  <step n="2" goal="Load project context and story information">
    <critical>Load all available context to inform implementation</critical>

    <action>Load {project_context} for coding standards and project-wide patterns (if exists)</action>
    <action>Parse sections: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Dev Agent Record, File List, Change Log, Status</action>
    <action>Load comprehensive context from story file's Dev Notes section</action>
    <action>Extract developer guidance from Dev Notes: architecture requirements, previous learnings, technical specifications</action>
    <action>Use enhanced story context to inform implementation decisions and approaches</action>
    <output>✅ **Context Loaded**
      Story and project context available for implementation
    </output>
  </step>

  <step n="3" goal="Detect review continuation and extract review context">
    <critical>Determine if this is a fresh start or continuation after code review</critical>

    <action>Check if "Senior Developer Review (AI)" section exists in the story file</action>
    <action>Check if "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection exists under Tasks/Subtasks</action>

    <check if="Senior Developer Review section exists">
      <action>Set review_continuation = true</action>
      <action>Extract from "Senior Developer Review (AI)" section:
        - Review outcome (Approve/Changes Requested/Blocked)
        - Review date
        - Total action items with checkboxes (count checked vs unchecked)
        - Severity breakdown (High/Med/Low counts)
      </action>
      <action>Count unchecked [ ] review follow-up tasks in "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection</action>
      <action>Store list of unchecked review items as {{pending_review_items}}</action>

      <output>⏯️ **Resuming Story After Code Review** ({{review_date}})

        **Review Outcome:** {{review_outcome}}
        **Action Items:** {{unchecked_review_count}} remaining to address
        **Priorities:** {{high_count}} High, {{med_count}} Medium, {{low_count}} Low

        **Strategy:** Will prioritize review follow-up tasks (marked [AI-Review]) before continuing with regular tasks.
      </output>
    </check>

    <check if="Senior Developer Review section does NOT exist">
      <action>Set review_continuation = false</action>
      <action>Set {{pending_review_items}} = empty</action>

      <output>🚀 **Starting Fresh Implementation**

        Story: {{story_key}}
        Story Status: {{current_status}}
        First incomplete task: {{first_task_description}}
      </output>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="4" goal="Mark story in-progress" tag="sprint-status">
    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file exists">
      <action>Load the FULL file: {{sprint_status}}</action>
      <action>Read all development_status entries to find {{story_key}}</action>
      <action>Get current status value for development_status[{{story_key}}]</action>

      <check if="current status == 'ready-for-dev' OR review_continuation == true">
        <action>Update the story in the sprint status report to = "in-progress"</action>
        <output>🚀 Starting work on story {{story_key}}
          Status updated: ready-for-dev → in-progress
        </output>
      </check>

      <check if="current status == 'in-progress'">
        <output>⏯️ Resuming work on story {{story_key}}
          Story is already marked in-progress
        </output>
      </check>

      <check if="current status is neither ready-for-dev nor in-progress">
        <output>⚠️ Unexpected story status: {{current_status}}
          Expected ready-for-dev or in-progress. Continuing anyway...
        </output>
      </check>

      <action>Store {{current_sprint_status}} for later use</action>
    </check>

    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file does NOT exist">
      <output>ℹ️ No sprint status file exists - story progress will be tracked in story file only</output>
      <action>Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "no-sprint-tracking"</action>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="5" goal="Implement task following red-green-refactor cycle">
    <critical>FOLLOW THE STORY FILE TASKS/SUBTASKS SEQUENCE EXACTLY AS WRITTEN - NO DEVIATION</critical>

    <action>Review the current task/subtask from the story file - this is your authoritative implementation guide</action>
    <action>Plan implementation following red-green-refactor cycle</action>

    <!-- RED PHASE -->
    <action>Write FAILING tests first for the task/subtask functionality</action>
    <action>Confirm tests fail before implementation - this validates test correctness</action>

    <!-- GREEN PHASE -->
    <action>Implement MINIMAL code to make tests pass</action>
    <action>Run tests to confirm they now pass</action>
    <action>Handle error conditions and edge cases as specified in task/subtask</action>

    <!-- REFACTOR PHASE -->
    <action>Improve code structure while keeping tests green</action>
    <action>Ensure code follows architecture patterns and coding standards from Dev Notes</action>

    <action>Document technical approach and decisions in Dev Agent Record → Implementation Plan</action>

    <action if="new dependencies required beyond story specifications">HALT: "Additional dependencies need user approval"</action>
    <action if="3 consecutive implementation failures occur">HALT and request guidance</action>
    <action if="required configuration is missing">HALT: "Cannot proceed without necessary configuration files"</action>

    <critical>NEVER implement anything not mapped to a specific task/subtask in the story file</critical>
    <critical>NEVER proceed to next task until current task/subtask is complete AND tests pass</critical>
    <critical>Execute continuously without pausing until all tasks/subtasks are complete or explicit HALT condition</critical>
    <critical>Do NOT propose to pause for review until Step 9 completion gates are satisfied</critical>
  </step>

  <step n="6" goal="Author comprehensive tests">
    <action>Create unit tests for business logic and core functionality introduced/changed by the task</action>
    <action>Add integration tests for component interactions specified in story requirements</action>
    <action>Include end-to-end tests for critical user flows when story requirements demand them</action>
    <action>Cover edge cases and error handling scenarios identified in story Dev Notes</action>
  </step>

  <step n="7" goal="Run validations and tests">
    <action>Determine how to run tests for this repo (infer test framework from project structure)</action>
    <action>Run all existing tests to ensure no regressions</action>
    <action>Run the new tests to verify implementation correctness</action>
    <action>Run linting and code quality checks if configured in project</action>
    <action>Validate implementation meets ALL story acceptance criteria; enforce quantitative thresholds explicitly</action>
    <action if="regression tests fail">STOP and fix before continuing - identify breaking changes immediately</action>
    <action if="new tests fail">STOP and fix before continuing - ensure implementation correctness</action>
  </step>

  <step n="8" goal="Validate and mark task complete ONLY when fully done">
    <critical>NEVER mark a task complete unless ALL conditions are met - NO LYING OR CHEATING</critical>

    <!-- VALIDATION GATES -->
    <action>Verify ALL tests for this task/subtask ACTUALLY EXIST and PASS 100%</action>
    <action>Confirm implementation matches EXACTLY what the task/subtask specifies - no extra features</action>
    <action>Validate that ALL acceptance criteria related to this task are satisfied</action>
    <action>Run full test suite to ensure NO regressions introduced</action>

    <!-- REVIEW FOLLOW-UP HANDLING -->
    <check if="task is review follow-up (has [AI-Review] prefix)">
      <action>Extract review item details (severity, description, related AC/file)</action>
      <action>Add to resolution tracking list: {{resolved_review_items}}</action>

      <!-- Mark task in Review Follow-ups section -->
      <action>Mark task checkbox [x] in "Tasks/Subtasks → Review Follow-ups (AI)" section</action>

      <!-- CRITICAL: Also mark corresponding action item in review section -->
      <action>Find matching action item in "Senior Developer Review (AI) → Action Items" section by matching description</action>
      <action>Mark that action item checkbox [x] as resolved</action>

      <action>Add to Dev Agent Record → Completion Notes: "✅ Resolved review finding [{{severity}}]: {{description}}"</action>
    </check>

    <!-- ONLY MARK COMPLETE IF ALL VALIDATION PASS -->
    <check if="ALL validation gates pass AND tests ACTUALLY exist and pass">
      <action>ONLY THEN mark the task (and subtasks) checkbox with [x]</action>
      <action>Update File List section with ALL new, modified, or deleted files (paths relative to repo root)</action>
      <action>Add completion notes to Dev Agent Record summarizing what was ACTUALLY implemented and tested</action>
    </check>

    <check if="ANY validation fails">
      <action>DO NOT mark task complete - fix issues first</action>
      <action>HALT if unable to fix validation failures</action>
    </check>

    <check if="review_continuation == true and {{resolved_review_items}} is not empty">
      <action>Count total resolved review items in this session</action>
      <action>Add Change Log entry: "Addressed code review findings - {{resolved_count}} items resolved (Date: {{date}})"</action>
    </check>

    <action>Save the story file</action>
    <action>Determine if more incomplete tasks remain</action>
    <action if="more tasks remain">
      <goto step="5">Next task</goto>
    </action>
    <action if="no tasks remain">
      <goto step="9">Completion</goto>
    </action>
  </step>

  <step n="9" goal="Story completion and mark for review" tag="sprint-status">
    <action>Verify ALL tasks and subtasks are marked [x] (re-scan the story document now)</action>
    <action>Run the full regression suite (do not skip)</action>
    <action>Confirm File List includes every changed file</action>
    <action>Execute enhanced definition-of-done validation</action>
    <action>Update the story Status to: "review"</action>

    <!-- Enhanced Definition of Done Validation -->
    <action>Validate definition-of-done checklist with essential requirements:
      - All tasks/subtasks marked complete with [x]
      - Implementation satisfies every Acceptance Criterion
      - Unit tests for core functionality added/updated
      - Integration tests for component interactions added when required
      - End-to-end tests for critical flows added when story demands them
      - All tests pass (no regressions, new tests successful)
      - Code quality checks pass (linting, static analysis if configured)
      - File List includes every new/modified/deleted file (relative paths)
      - Dev Agent Record contains implementation notes
      - Change Log includes summary of changes
      - Only permitted story sections were modified
    </action>

    <!-- Mark story ready for review - sprint status conditional -->
    <check if="{sprint_status} file exists AND {{current_sprint_status}} != 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <action>Load the FULL file: {sprint_status}</action>
      <action>Find development_status key matching {{story_key}}</action>
      <action>Verify current status is "in-progress" (expected previous state)</action>
      <action>Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "review"</action>
      <action>Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure including STATUS DEFINITIONS</action>
      <output>✅ Story status updated to "review" in sprint-status.yaml</output>
    </check>

    <check if="{sprint_status} file does NOT exist OR {{current_sprint_status}} == 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <output>ℹ️ Story status updated to "review" in story file (no sprint tracking configured)</output>
    </check>

    <check if="story key not found in sprint status">
      <output>⚠️ Story file updated, but sprint-status update failed: {{story_key}} not found

        Story status is set to "review" in file, but sprint-status.yaml may be out of sync.
      </output>
    </check>

    <!-- Final validation gates -->
    <action if="any task is incomplete">HALT - Complete remaining tasks before marking ready for review</action>
    <action if="regression failures exist">HALT - Fix regression issues before completing</action>
    <action if="File List is incomplete">HALT - Update File List with all changed files</action>
    <action if="definition-of-done validation fails">HALT - Address DoD failures before completing</action>
  </step>

  <step n="10" goal="Completion communication and user support">
    <action>Execute the enhanced definition-of-done checklist using the validation framework</action>
    <action>Prepare a concise summary in Dev Agent Record → Completion Notes</action>

    <action>Communicate to {user_name} that story implementation is complete and ready for review</action>
    <action>Summarize key accomplishments: story ID, story key, title, key changes made, tests added, files modified</action>
    <action>Provide the story file path and current status (now "review")</action>

    <action>Based on {user_skill_level}, ask if user needs any explanations about:
      - What was implemented and how it works
      - Why certain technical decisions were made
      - How to test or verify the changes
      - Any patterns, libraries, or approaches used
      - Anything else they'd like clarified
    </action>

    <check if="user asks for explanations">
      <action>Provide clear, contextual explanations tailored to {user_skill_level}</action>
      <action>Use examples and references to specific code when helpful</action>
    </check>

    <action>Once explanations are complete (or user indicates no questions), suggest logical next steps</action>
    <action>Recommended next steps (flexible based on project setup):
      - Review the implemented story and test the changes
      - Verify all acceptance criteria are met
      - Ensure deployment readiness if applicable
      - Run `code-review` workflow for peer review
      - Optional: If Test Architect module installed, run `/bmad:tea:automate` to expand guardrail tests
    </action>

    <output>💡 **Tip:** For best results, run `code-review` using a **different** LLM than the one that implemented this story.</output>
    <check if="{sprint_status} file exists">
      <action>Suggest checking {sprint_status} to see project progress</action>
    </check>
    <action>Remain flexible - allow user to choose their own path or ask for other assistance</action>
  </step>

</workflow>

   120→## Epic 1: Working Quota Submission
   121→
   122→After this epic, a DevOps engineer can execute the script and submit real Azure Quota API requests for all subscriptions defined in `quota-config.json`. The simulated API call is replaced with a working `az rest` integration, and subscription iteration is driven by the config file rather than `az account list`.
   123→
   124→### Story 1.1: Config-Driven Subscription Iteration
   125→
   126→As a DevOps engineer,
   127→I want the script to iterate over subscriptions defined in my config file,
   128→So that only the subscriptions I explicitly configure receive quota requests.
   129→
   130→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   131→
   132→**Given** a valid `quota-config.json` with 3 subscriptions in the `subscriptions` array
   133→**When** the script executes the main processing loop
   134→**Then** it iterates exactly over those 3 subscriptions (not all subscriptions from `az account list`)
   135→**And** each subscription's `id` and `name` are correctly extracted from the config
   136→
   137→**Given** a `quota-config.json` with 1 subscription in the array
   138→**When** the script executes
   139→**Then** only that single subscription is processed
   140→
   141→**Given** the main processing loop uses process substitution (`while read -r sub; do ... done < <(...)`)
   142→**When** variables are incremented inside the loop body
   143→**Then** those variable values persist after the loop exits (no subshell scoping issue)
   144→
   145→**Implementation Notes:**
   146→- Replace `az account list` call (line 119) with `jq -c '.subscriptions[]' "$CONFIG_FILE"`
   147→- Use process substitution pattern: `while read -r sub; do ... done < <(echo "$config" | jq -c '.subscriptions[]')`
   148→- Remove the `sub_count` variable that currently reads from `az account list`
   149→- Set `sub_count` from config: `local sub_count=$(echo "$config" | jq '.subscriptions | length')`
   150→
   151→### Story 1.2: Azure Quota API Integration
   152→
   153→As a DevOps engineer,
   154→I want the script to submit real quota increase requests to the Azure Quota API,
   155→So that I no longer need to manually submit requests through the Azure portal.
   156→
   157→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   158→
   159→**Given** the script is authenticated via `az login` and a subscription context is set
   160→**When** `submit_quota_request()` is called for a subscription
   161→**Then** it calls `az rest` with the correct PUT method, URI, and JSON request body
   162→**And** the URI follows the pattern: `https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{sub_id}/providers/Microsoft.Capacity/resourceProviders/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/locations/{region}/serviceLimits/{resource_name}?api-version=2023-02-01`
   163→
   164→**Given** config values for model, region, capacity, and requestor details
   165→**When** the JSON request body is constructed
   166→**Then** it is built using `jq -n` with `--arg` parameters (never string concatenation)
   167→**And** all required fields from the config are included in the body
   168→
   169→**Given** the API returns HTTP 200, 201, or 202
   170→**When** the response is received
   171→**Then** the function returns exit code 0 (success)
   172→**And** a SUCCESS log message is written with the subscription name
   173→
   174→**Given** the API returns an error response
   175→**When** the response is received
   176→**Then** the function returns exit code 1 (failure)
   177→**And** the error message from the response is logged at ERROR level
   178→
   179→**Given** the `az account set` call fails for a subscription
   180→**When** the subscription context cannot be set
   181→**Then** the function returns exit code 1 without attempting the API call
   182→**And** a WARN log message identifies the subscription
   183→
   184→**Implementation Notes:**
   185→- Replace the simulated block (lines 97-100) with actual `az rest` call
   186→- Build request body with `jq -n --arg` for each config field
   187→- Capture response: `response=$(az rest ... 2>&1) && http_code=$? || http_code=$?`
   188→- Never log PII fields (firstName, lastName, email, address) — only subscription ID/name and model parameters
   189→
   190→### Story 1.3: API Response Logging and Validation
   191→
   192→As a DevOps engineer,
   193→I want to see detailed API response information in the logs,
   194→So that I can diagnose issues and provide audit evidence of submissions.
   195→
   196→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   197→
   198→**Given** a successful API response (200/201/202)
   199→**When** the response is logged
   200→**Then** the log entry includes the subscription name, model, region, and a status indicator
   201→**And** the response body (or relevant fields) is logged at INFO level
   202→**And** no PII fields from the config appear in the log
   203→
   204→**Given** a failed API response with an error message
   205→**When** the response is logged
   206→**Then** the log entry includes the subscription name, HTTP status context, and the error message
   207→**And** the error is logged at ERROR level with the RED color code on console
   208→
   209→**Given** the log file `quota-requests-YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log` is generated
   210→**When** a team lead opens it for audit review
   211→**Then** every API call attempt is recorded with timestamp, subscription name, and outcome
   212→**And** no PII (name, email, address, company) appears anywhere in the file
   213→
   214→**Implementation Notes:**
   215→- Log API call attempt before making it: `log "INFO" "Submitting quota request for model: $model in region: $region"`
   216→- Log response after receiving it (success or failure)
   217→- Filter PII: only log `sub_id`, `sub_name`, `model`, `region`, `capacity` — never `firstName`, `lastName`, `email`, `address`, `company`
   218→
   219→---
   220→
   221→## Epic 2: Reliable Reporting & Operational Hardening
   222→
   223→After this epic, the operator receives accurate success/failure counts in the summary, the retry logic intelligently distinguishes transient from permanent failures, exit codes correctly reflect batch outcomes, and the tool includes pre-flight auth validation and security hardening for production use.
   224→
   225→### Story 2.1: Fix Counter Accuracy with Process Substitution
   226→
   227→As a DevOps engineer,
   228→I want the execution summary to accurately report how many subscriptions succeeded and failed,
   229→So that I can trust the tool's output without manually counting log entries.
   230→
   231→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   232→
   233→**Given** 3 subscriptions are configured and all 3 succeed
   234→**When** the execution summary is displayed
   235→**Then** `Successful: 3` and `Failed: 0` are reported correctly
   236→
   237→**Given** 3 subscriptions are configured and 1 fails after all retries
   238→**When** the execution summary is displayed
   239→**Then** `Successful: 2` and `Failed: 1` are reported correctly
   240→
   241→**Given** the subscription iteration loop uses process substitution
   242→**When** `success_count` and `failed_count` are incremented inside the loop
   243→**Then** their values are preserved after the loop exits (main shell scope, not subshell)
   244→
   245→**Implementation Notes:**
   246→- This fix is architecturally integrated with Story 1.1 (process substitution pattern)
   247→- If Story 1.1 is already complete, verify that counter increments work correctly in the new loop structure
   248→- The `success_count` and `failed_count` variables must be declared before the loop and readable after it
   249→
   250→### Story 2.2: Transient vs Permanent Error Classification
   251→
   252→As a DevOps engineer,
   253→I want the retry logic to only retry transient failures and skip permanent ones immediately,
   254→So that the script doesn't waste time retrying errors that will never succeed.
   255→
   256→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   257→
   258→**Given** the API returns HTTP 429 (rate limit), 503 (service unavailable), or 504 (gateway timeout)
   259→**When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
   260→**Then** it retries the request up to 3 times with a 5-second delay between attempts
   261→**And** each retry attempt is logged at WARN level with the attempt number
   262→
   263→**Given** the API returns HTTP 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 403 (forbidden), or 404 (not found)
   264→**When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
   265→**Then** it does NOT retry and immediately marks the subscription as failed
   266→**And** the permanent error is logged at ERROR level with the specific error message
   267→
   268→**Given** a network error occurs (connection refused, DNS failure, timeout)
   269→**When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
   270→**Then** it treats this as a transient error and retries
   271→
   272→**Given** a transient error persists after 3 retry attempts
   273→**When** all retries are exhausted
   274→**Then** the subscription is marked as failed with a final ERROR log message
   275→**And** processing continues to the next subscription (NFR7 fault isolation)
   276→
   277→**Implementation Notes:**
   278→- `az rest` returns non-zero exit code on failure — parse stderr for HTTP status codes
   279→- Extract HTTP code from error output to classify as transient vs permanent
   280→- Transient codes: 429, 503, 504, network errors
   281→- Permanent codes: 400, 401, 403, 404, 409
   282→
   283→### Story 2.3: Exit Code Logic and Summary Reporting
   284→
   285→As a DevOps engineer running this script in a CI/CD pipeline,
   286→I want the script to exit with code 0 only when all subscriptions succeed,
   287→So that my pipeline correctly detects partial or complete failures.
   288→
   289→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   290→
   291→**Given** all configured subscriptions succeed
   292→**When** the script finishes execution
   293→**Then** it exits with code 0
   294→**And** the summary shows all green SUCCESS messages
   295→
   296→**Given** one or more subscriptions fail after all retries
   297→**When** the script finishes execution
   298→**Then** it exits with code 1
   299→**And** the summary clearly shows the count of failed subscriptions in RED
   300→
   301→**Given** the execution summary is displayed
   302→**When** the user reads the output
   303→**Then** it shows: `Successful: N | Failed: M` on a single line
   304→**And** the total matches the number of configured subscriptions
   305→
   306→**Implementation Notes:**
   307→- Add exit code logic after the summary block:
   308→  ```bash
   309→  if [[ $failed_count -gt 0 ]]; then
   310→      exit 1
   311→  fi
   312→  ```
   313→- Ensure this runs AFTER the summary is printed (not before)
   314→
   315→### Story 2.4: Pre-Flight Auth Check and Environment Validation
   316→
   317→As a new team member setting up the tool for the first time,
   318→I want the script to clearly tell me if my Azure session is expired or not configured,
   319→So that I can fix it before the script attempts (and fails) to process subscriptions.
   320→
   321→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   322→
   323→**Given** the user has a valid `az login` session
   324→**When** the script runs the pre-flight checks
   325→**Then** it passes the auth validation and proceeds to processing
   326→
   327→**Given** the user's `az login` session has expired or was never created
   328→**When** the script runs the pre-flight checks
   329→**Then** it exits with a clear error message: "Azure CLI session is not active. Please run 'az login' first."
   330→**And** exits with code 1 before attempting any subscription processing
   331→
   332→**Given** all existing pre-flight checks (FR6-FR8, FR15-FR16) are already implemented
   333→**When** the script runs
   334→**Then** config file path resolution, JSON validation, and dependency checks all function correctly
   335→**And** the new auth check runs after dependency checks but before subscription processing
   336→
   337→**Implementation Notes:**
   338→- Add `az account show &>/dev/null || error_exit "Azure CLI session is not active. Please run 'az login' first."` after `check_dependencies()` and `validate_config()`
   339→- This validates FR17 and provides a clear error message per FR16's pattern
   340→
   341→### Story 2.5: Security Hardening and Repository Hygiene
   342→
   343→As a DevOps engineer working in a team repository,
   344→I want sensitive files to be excluded from version control and a safe config template to be available,
   345→So that PII and log files are never accidentally committed.
   346→
   347→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   348→
   349→**Given** the repository has a `.gitignore` file
   350→**When** a developer creates log files or has a local `quota-config.json`
   351→**Then** `quota-requests-*.log` files are excluded from git tracking
   352→**And** `quota-config.json` is excluded from git tracking
   353→**And** `.envrc` is excluded from git tracking
   354→
   355→**Given** a new developer clones the repository
   356→**When** they need to create their `quota-config.json`
   357→**Then** `quota-config.example.json` is available with the correct schema structure
   358→**And** it contains placeholder values (no real subscription IDs, emails, or PII)
   359→**And** comments or field names make it clear what each field requires
   360→
   361→**Given** the `quota-config.example.json` template
   362→**When** a developer copies it to `quota-config.json`
   363→**Then** they only need to fill in their actual subscription IDs and requestor details
   364→**And** the file structure matches what the script expects
   365→
   366→**Implementation Notes:**
   367→- Create `.gitignore` with: `quota-requests-*.log`, `quota-config.json`, `.envrc`
   368→- Create `quota-config.example.json` with placeholder values:
   369→  - `"id": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"`
   370→  - `"email": "your-email@company.com"`
   371→  - All other fields with descriptive placeholders
   372→- NFR1: Document `chmod 600 quota-config.json` in the example file header or SETUP.md

# azure-quota-automation - Epic Breakdown

## Overview

This document provides the complete epic and story breakdown for azure-quota-automation, decomposing the requirements from the PRD and Architecture into implementable stories. This is a brownfield enhancement of an existing 161-line Bash script — many FRs are already implemented and only require validation, not new development.

## Requirements Inventory

### Functional Requirements

**Quota Request Submission:**
- FR1: Operator can submit quota increase requests to the Azure Quota API for each configured subscription
- FR2: Operator can specify target model, provider, region, and capacity in a JSON configuration file
- FR3: System retries failed quota submissions up to 3 times with a 5-second delay between attempts
- FR4: System sets the active Azure subscription context before each submission
- FR5: System processes all configured subscriptions sequentially in a single execution

**Configuration Management:**
- FR6: Operator can provide a custom configuration file path as a command-line argument *(existing)*
- FR7: System defaults to `quota-config.json` in the working directory when no argument is provided *(existing)*
- FR8: System validates JSON syntax of the configuration file before processing *(existing)*
- FR9: System reads requestor identity, Azure target parameters, and subscription list from the configuration file

**Reporting & Logging:**
- FR10: System logs every operation with timestamp, level (INFO/WARN/ERROR), and message to both console and log file *(existing)*
- FR11: System generates a timestamped log file (`quota-requests-YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log`) per execution *(existing)*
- FR12: System displays color-coded output on the console (blue=INFO, green=SUCCESS, yellow=WARN, red=ERROR) *(existing)*
- FR13: System reports accurate success and failure counts in the execution summary
- FR14: System exits with code 0 when all subscriptions succeed and non-zero when any fail

**Dependency & Environment Validation:**
- FR15: System validates that required CLI tools (az, jq, curl) are installed before processing *(existing)*
- FR16: System exits with a clear error message identifying any missing dependency *(existing)*
- FR17: System validates the Azure CLI authentication session is active before processing subscriptions

### NonFunctional Requirements

**Security:**
- NFR1: Configuration file containing PII (name, email, address) must be protected with file permissions (chmod 600)
- NFR2: No credentials, tokens, or secrets are stored in the script or configuration file *(existing)*
- NFR3: Azure authentication relies exclusively on the user's `az login` session *(existing)*
- NFR4: Log files must not contain sensitive fields (passwords, tokens); subscription IDs are acceptable for audit purposes

**Reliability:**
- NFR5: Script uses `set -euo pipefail` to fail fast on errors, undefined variables, and pipe failures *(existing)*
- NFR6: Retry logic handles transient API failures (network timeouts, rate limiting) for up to 3 attempts per subscription
- NFR7: A single subscription failure does not halt processing of remaining subscriptions

**Performance:**
- NFR8: Script completes processing of 3 subscriptions within 60 seconds under normal network conditions *(existing)*
- NFR9: Retry delays are fixed at 5 seconds between attempts *(existing)*

**Integration:**
- NFR10: Script is compatible with macOS (Bash 4.0+) and Linux environments *(existing)*
- NFR11: Script operates with Azure CLI latest stable version *(existing)*
- NFR12: Script requires only standard Unix utilities (jq, curl) plus Azure CLI as external dependencies *(existing)*

### Additional Requirements

**From Architecture Document:**

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**When** the execution summary is displayed
**Then** `Successful: 2` and `Failed: 1` are reported correctly

**Given** the subscription iteration loop uses process substitution
**When** `success_count` and `failed_count` are incremented inside the loop
**Then** their values are preserved after the loop exits (main shell scope, not subshell)

### Story 2.2: Transient vs Permanent Error Classification

As a DevOps engineer,
I want the retry logic to only retry transient failures and skip permanent ones immediately,
So that the script doesn't waste time retrying errors that will never succeed.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the API returns HTTP 429 (rate limit), 503 (service unavailable), or 504 (gateway timeout)
**When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
**Then** it retries the request up to 3 times with a 5-second delay between attempts
**And** each retry attempt is logged at WARN level with the attempt number

**Given** the API returns HTTP 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 403 (forbidden), or 404 (not found)
**When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
**Then** it does NOT retry and immediately marks the subscription as failed
**And** the permanent error is logged at ERROR level with the specific error message

**Given** a network error occurs (connection refused, DNS failure, timeout)
**When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
**Then** it treats this as a transient error and retries

**Given** a transient error persists after 3 retry attempts
**When** all retries are exhausted
**Then** the subscription is marked as failed with a final ERROR log message
**And** processing continues to the next subscription (NFR7 fault isolation)

**Implementation Notes:**
- `az rest` returns non-zero exit code on failure — parse stderr for HTTP status codes
- Extract HTTP code from error output to classify as transient vs permanent
- Transient codes: 429, 503, 504, network errors
- Permanent codes: 400, 401, 403, 404, 409

### Story 2.3: Exit Code Logic and Summary Reporting

As a DevOps engineer running this script in a CI/CD pipeline,
I want the script to exit with code 0 only when all subscriptions succeed,
So that my pipeline correctly detects partial or complete failures.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** all configured subscriptions succeed

**Then** it exits with code 0

**Given** one or more subscriptions fail after all retries

**Given** the execution summary is displayed
**When** the user reads the output
**Then** it shows: `Successful: N | Failed: M` on a single line
**And** the total matches the number of configured subscriptions

**Implementation Notes:**

- Ensure this runs AFTER the summary is printed (not before)

# Story 2.2: Transient vs Permanent Error Classification

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a DevOps engineer,
I want the retry logic to only retry transient failures and skip permanent ones immediately,
so that the script doesn't waste time retrying errors that will never succeed.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** the API returns HTTP 429 (rate limit), 503 (service unavailable), or 504 (gateway timeout)
   **When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
   **Then** it retries the request up to 3 times with a 5-second delay between attempts
   **And** each retry attempt is logged at WARN level with the attempt number

2. **Given** the API returns HTTP 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 403 (forbidden), or 404 (not found)
   **When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
   **Then** it does NOT retry and immediately marks the subscription as failed
   **And** the permanent error is logged at ERROR level with the specific error message

3. **Given** a network error occurs (connection refused, DNS failure, timeout)
   **When** the retry logic evaluates the failure
   **Then** it treats this as a transient error and retries

4. **Given** a transient error persists after 3 retry attempts
   **When** all retries are exhausted
   **Then** the subscription is marked as failed with a final ERROR log message
   **And** processing continues to the next subscription (NFR7 fault isolation)

5. **Given** a permanent error (e.g., 403 Forbidden)
   **When** the error is logged
   **Then** the log entry includes the subscription name, error code, and specific error message from the API response

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Add `classify_error` helper function (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [ ] 1.1: Create `classify_error()` function that takes a response string and returns "transient", "permanent", or "unknown"
  - [ ] 1.2: Parse HTTP status codes from `az rest` error output using grep/sed pattern matching
  - [ ] 1.3: Map transient codes (429, 503, 504) → return "transient"
  - [ ] 1.4: Map permanent codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 409) → return "permanent"
  - [ ] 1.5: Default: if no HTTP code extracted (network error, timeout) → return "transient"
- [ ] Task 2: Modify `submit_quota_request` return codes (AC: #1, #2, #3, #5)
  - [ ] 2.1: Return 0 = success (unchanged)
  - [ ] 2.2: Return 1 = permanent failure (do not retry)
  - [ ] 2.3: Return 2 = transient failure (retry eligible)
  - [ ] 2.4: Call `classify_error` on the error response text to determine return code
  - [ ] 2.5: Log the error classification in the ERROR log message (e.g., "permanent error — not retrying")
- [ ] Task 3: Update retry loop in `main()` to respect error classification (AC: #1, #2, #4)
  - [ ] 3.1: Capture return code from `submit_quota_request` (not just success/fail)
  - [ ] 3.2: Return 0 → success_count++, break (unchanged)
  - [ ] 3.3: Return 1 (permanent) → failed_count++, break immediately (NO retry)
  - [ ] 3.4: Return 2 (transient) → retry if attempts remain, else failed_count++
  - [ ] 3.5: Log WARN with attempt number on each transient retry
  - [ ] 3.6: Log ERROR with "all retries exhausted" when transient error persists after MAX_RETRIES
- [ ] Task 4: Verify counter invariant still holds (AC: #4)
  - [ ] 4.1: Every subscription must increment exactly one counter (success_count OR failed_count)
  - [ ] 4.2: Trace all exit paths: success, permanent-fail, transient-exhausted, malformed-sub
  - [ ] 4.3: `success_count + failed_count == sub_count` must hold for all executions
- [ ] Task 5: Static analysis and testing (all AC)
  - [ ] 5.1: Run `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — no syntax errors
  - [ ] 5.2: Run ShellCheck — no warnings
  - [ ] 5.3: Logic trace: 3 subs, all succeed → Successful: 3, Failed: 0
  - [ ] 5.4: Logic trace: 1 permanent fail, 2 succeed → Successful: 2, Failed: 1 (no retries on permanent)
  - [ ] 5.5: Logic trace: 1 transient fail exhausts 3 retries, 2 succeed → Successful: 2, Failed: 1

## Dev Notes

### Critical Implementation Context

**This story modifies the retry logic to be intelligent about error types.** Currently (line 236), the retry loop retries ALL failures indiscriminately — a 403 Forbidden gets 3 retry attempts that will all fail identically. This wastes 10+ seconds per permanent failure.

**Approach: Return-code-based error classification.**
- `submit_quota_request()` returns **0** (success), **1** (permanent), or **2** (transient)
- The retry loop in `main()` checks the return code to decide whether to retry
- A new `classify_error()` helper parses the `az rest` error output to extract HTTP status codes

### How `az rest` Reports Errors

When `az rest` fails, the current code captures the output at line 158:
```bash
response=$(az rest --method PUT --uri "$uri" --body "$request_body" --only-show-errors 2>&1) && exit_code=$? || exit_code=$?
```

**Error output formats from `az rest`:**

1. **HTTP API errors** (JSON body): `az rest` may output the Azure error JSON:
   ```json
   {"error": {"code": "TooManyRequests", "message": "Rate limit exceeded..."}}
   ```
   Common `error.code` values:
   - `BadRequest`, `InvalidParameter` → 400
   - `AuthenticationFailed`, `InvalidAuthenticationToken` → 401
   - `AuthorizationFailed`, `Forbidden` → 403
   - `ResourceNotFound`, `NotFound` → 404
   - `Conflict` → 409
   - `TooManyRequests`, `RequestRateLimitExceeded` → 429

2. **CLI-level errors** (plain text, `az rest` wraps HTTP errors):
   ```
   (HttpResponsePayloadError) Response status code is not successful: '429 (Too Many Requests)'
   ```
   This pattern is the most reliable for extracting HTTP status codes.

3. **Network errors** (plain text):
   ```
   Connection refused
   Could not resolve host
   Operation timed out
   ```
   No HTTP code extractable — classify as transient.

### Classification Strategy

Use a **two-tier approach** for maximum robustness:

1. **Primary:** Extract HTTP status code from `az rest` error text using regex pattern:
   ```bash
   # Match patterns like "status code is not successful: '429" or just "429"
   http_code=$(echo "$response" | grep -oP "(?:status code.*?')\K[0-9]{3}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   ```

2. **Fallback:** If no HTTP code found, check `error.code` from JSON:
   ```bash
   error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   ```
   Map known error codes to transient/permanent.

3. **Default:** If neither extraction works (network error) → classify as **transient** (safer to retry).

### Transient vs Permanent Classification Table

| HTTP Code | error.code | Classification | Rationale |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|-----------|
| 429 | TooManyRequests | **TRANSIENT** | Rate limiting, will clear |
| 503 | ServiceUnavailable | **TRANSIENT** | Temporary outage |
| 504 | GatewayTimeout | **TRANSIENT** | Temporary connectivity |
| 500 | InternalServerError | **TRANSIENT** | Server-side, may recover |
| 400 | BadRequest | **PERMANENT** | Invalid request, won't change |
| 401 | AuthenticationFailed | **PERMANENT** | Credentials issue |
| 403 | AuthorizationFailed | **PERMANENT** | Permission issue |
| 404 | ResourceNotFound | **PERMANENT** | Wrong endpoint/resource |
| 409 | Conflict | **PERMANENT** | State conflict |
| (none) | (none) | **TRANSIENT** | Network error, retry |

**Note:** 500 (InternalServerError) is added to transient list even though the epics only specify 429/503/504 — server errors are typically transient. However, the architecture doc only lists 429, 503, 504 as transient. **Follow the architecture doc strictly:** only 429, 503, 504 + network errors are transient. 500 is NOT in the transient list per architecture decision.

### Exact Code Changes Required

**File:** `submit-quota-requests.sh` (currently 267 lines)

#### Change 1: Add `classify_error()` function (insert after `error_exit()`, before `check_dependencies()`)

Insert at line ~46 (after `error_exit` function, before `check_dependencies`):
```bash
# Classify API error as transient (retry) or permanent (fail immediately)
# Returns: "transient" or "permanent"
classify_error() {
    local response=$1

    # Primary: extract HTTP status code from az rest error text
    local http_code
    http_code=$(echo "$response" | grep -oE '[0-9]{3} \(' | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]{3}' || echo "")

    if [[ -n "$http_code" ]]; then
        case "$http_code" in
            429|503|504) echo "transient"; return ;;
            400|401|403|404|409) echo "permanent"; return ;;
        esac
    fi

    # Fallback: check error.code from JSON response
    local error_code
    error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""

    case "$error_code" in
        TooManyRequests|RequestRateLimitExceeded|ServiceUnavailable|GatewayTimeout)
            echo "transient"; return ;;
        BadRequest|InvalidParameter|AuthenticationFailed|InvalidAuthenticationToken|AuthorizationFailed|Forbidden|ResourceNotFound|NotFound|Conflict)
            echo "permanent"; return ;;
    esac

    # Default: if we can't determine, assume transient (safer to retry)
    echo "transient"
}
```

#### Change 2: Modify `submit_quota_request()` return codes (lines 160-181)

Replace the current error handling block (lines 166-181):
```bash
    else
        # Extract error code and message safely — avoid logging raw response which may contain PII
        local error_code
        local error_msg
        error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""
        error_msg=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.message // .message // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_msg=""
        if [[ -z "$error_msg" ]]; then
            error_msg="Non-JSON error response from az rest (check Azure CLI output)"
        fi
        local error_context="${error_code:+[$error_code] }${error_msg:-Unknown error}"
        error_context="${error_context:0:300}"

        # Classify error for retry decision
        local error_class
        error_class=$(classify_error "$response")

        if [[ "$error_class" == "permanent" ]]; then
            log "ERROR" "${RED}FAILED (permanent — will not retry): $sub_name (model: $model, region: $region) - ${error_context}${NC}"
            return 1
        else
            log "WARN" "${YELLOW}FAILED (transient — eligible for retry): $sub_name (model: $model, region: $region) - ${error_context}${NC}"
            return 2
        fi
    fi
```

#### Change 3: Update retry loop in `main()` (lines 234-250)

Replace the current retry loop:
```bash
        # Try to submit quota request with retries (error-classified)
        retry_count=0
        local rc=0
        while [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
            submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$config" && rc=0 || rc=$?

            if [[ $rc -eq 0 ]]; then
                # Success
                success_count=$((success_count + 1))
                break
            elif [[ $rc -eq 1 ]]; then
                # Permanent failure — do not retry
                failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
                break
            else
                # Transient failure (rc=2) — retry if attempts remain
                retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
                if [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
                    log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Retry $retry_count/$MAX_RETRIES for $sub_name in ${RETRY_DELAY}s...${NC}"
                    sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
                else
                    failed_count=$((failed_count + 1))
                    log "ERROR" "${RED}Failed after $MAX_RETRIES transient retries for $sub_name${NC}"
                fi
            fi
        done
```

### Counter Exit Path Analysis (After Changes)

Every subscription MUST increment exactly one counter. Updated paths:

| Path | Counter | Condition |
|------|---------|-----------|
| Malformed sub (missing id/name) | `failed_count++` | Line ~230 (existing) |
| Success on attempt 1-3 | `success_count++` | rc=0 → break |
| Permanent failure | `failed_count++` | rc=1 → break (no retry) |
| Transient exhaustion (3 retries) | `failed_count++` | rc=2 + retry_count == MAX_RETRIES |

**Invariant:** `success_count + failed_count == sub_count` for all executions.

### Architecture Compliance

| Rule | How This Story Complies |
|------|------------------------|
| Process substitution for loops | Unchanged: `done < <(echo "$config" \| jq -c '.subscriptions[]')` |
| Safe arithmetic under `set -e` | All counters use `$((var + 1))` not `((var++))` |
| `snake_case` functions and variables | `classify_error`, `error_class`, `http_code`, `error_code` |
| `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants | `MAX_RETRIES`, `RETRY_DELAY` (unchanged) |
| Preserve `set -euo pipefail` | No `set +e` escapes; function return codes captured via `&& rc=0 \|\| rc=$?` |
| Use `jq` for JSON parsing | JSON error fields parsed with `jq -r` |
| Never log PII fields | Only log error_code, error_msg (API error details, not PII) |
| No new dependencies | Uses only existing `grep`, `jq`, Bash builtins |

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- **DO NOT** use `set +e` to capture return codes — use `command && rc=0 || rc=$?` pattern
- **DO NOT** log the raw `response` variable — it may contain PII or sensitive data from Azure CLI output
- **DO NOT** change `submit_quota_request()` function signature — same 3 args (sub_id, sub_name, config)
- **DO NOT** modify counter declarations or process substitution loop structure
- **DO NOT** modify `log()`, `error_exit()`, `check_dependencies()`, `validate_config()`
- **DO NOT** add `curl`-based retry (use `az rest` exclusively per architecture decision)
- **DO NOT** use `eval` or backtick command substitution
- **DO NOT** use `grep` for JSON parsing — use `jq`
- **DO NOT** retry on `az account set` failure (line 127) — subscription context failures are permanent
- **DO NOT** change the `--only-show-errors` flag on the `az rest` call

### What NOT to Touch

- `log()` function (lines 29-38) — working correctly
- `error_exit()` function (lines 41-44) — working correctly
- `check_dependencies()` function (lines 47-58) — working correctly
- `validate_config()` function (lines 61-99) — working correctly
- Configuration constants (lines 11-19) — unchanged

- Auth pre-flight check (lines 193-196) — working correctly
- Summary block (lines 254-258) — unchanged

- Counter declarations at lines 217-218 — unchanged

### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 2.1)

**Key learnings from Story 2.1 (verification-only):**

### Latest Technical Information

**Azure CLI `az rest` error behavior (Azure CLI 2.67+):**
- On HTTP errors, `az rest` outputs error JSON to stderr (captured by `2>&1` in current code)
- The `--only-show-errors` flag suppresses warnings but keeps error output
- HTTP status code often appears in CLI error text like: `Response status code is not successful: '429 (Too Many Requests)'`
- The `error.code` field in JSON responses uses Azure-standard error codes (e.g., `TooManyRequests`, `AuthorizationFailed`)
- Network failures produce plain-text errors without HTTP codes

**Azure Quota API (`api-version=2023-02-01`) common error responses:**
- 429: Rate limit — `Retry-After` header may be present (not used by this script; fixed 5s delay)
- 403: Missing RBAC role assignment on subscription
- 400: Invalid resource_name or malformed request body
- 404: Wrong provider path or non-existent resource

   121→### Counter Exit Path Analysis
   122→
   123→Every subscription MUST increment exactly one counter. The paths are:
   124→
   125→| Path | Counter | Line | Condition |
   126→|------|---------|------|-----------|
   127→| Malformed subscription (missing id/name) | `failed_count++` | 230 | Validation fails → continue to next sub |
   128→| Success on attempt 1-3 | `success_count++` | 238 | `submit_quota_request` returns 0 → break |
   129→| Failure after all 3 retries | `failed_count++` | 246 | `retry_count == MAX_RETRIES` in else branch |
   130→
   131→**Verified:** Every subscription increments exactly one counter. The `while [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]` loop condition prevents double-counting — when `retry_count == MAX_RETRIES`, the inner while exits after incrementing `failed_count`. The malformed-sub path increments `failed_count` and `continue`s before entering the retry loop.
